meta.sdr_profile: High football IQ — played or coached
meta.selling_season: Year-round — summer is primary window (install, camp prep)

positioning.one_liner: XIQ helps football staffs replace the scattered mix of Excel, Google Sheets, PDFs, Hudl notes, and manually updated wristbands with one organized system for game-week prep.
positioning.vs_hudl: Hudl is film. XIQ is the offensive planning layer around the film.
positioning.not: XIQ is not a Hudl replacement.
positioning.is: Play inventory, practice scripts, call sheets, wristbands, self-scout — one place.

head_coach.label: Head Football Coach
head_coach.primary_goal: Book 15-minute walkthrough with HC or get referred to OC/QB coach who owns call sheet, script, and wristband workflow
pain_points: Keeping wristbands updated without manual rework
Self-scouting by down, distance, personnel, formation, concept

opener.label: Opener — Pattern Interrupt
opener.script:
Coach [Last Name], this is [Your Name] with XIQ — I'll be honest with you, I'm cold calling.

We work with programs at Alabama, Baylor, Hawaii — and a lot of high school staffs — on one specific problem: game-week prep is still scattered across Excel, PDFs, and manual wristbands even when you have Hudl.

Real quick — who owns your call sheet and wristband workflow, you or your OC?
opener.note: Pattern interrupt disarms the guard. College names land before they can hang up. One question closes the opener — no pitch yet.

coach_owns_workflow.label: Coach owns the workflow
coach_owns_workflow.script:
Perfect.

The reason I'm calling now — summer — is that this is actually the best window to look at it. Once game week starts, nobody has time. But right now, before camp, while you're still building your install, is when staffs can actually evaluate something and get the workflow set up before it matters.

Most staffs have Hudl for film, but the rest of game-week prep is still manual. Call sheet in one place, wristband in Excel, practice script separate, self-scout takes extra time or doesn't get done consistently.

How are you guys building your call sheet and wristbands right now?
coach_owns_workflow.note: Stop and listen. Their answer tells you everything.

coach_refers_to_oc.label: Coach refers to OC
coach_refers_to_oc.script:
Got it — that makes sense.

Is Coach [OC Last Name] the right person for call sheet, script, and wristband workflow? I'm not trying to go around you — just want to make sure I'm talking to whoever actually owns game-week prep.

[If yes]: Perfect. What's the best way to reach him? And would it be okay if I mention I spoke with you briefly?

coach_what_is_xiq.label: Coach asks what XIQ is
coach_what_is_xiq.script:
Sure — short version:

XIQ is a football operations platform. It gives the staff one place to manage play inventory, build the weekly practice script, organize the call sheet, print wristbands, and run self-scout.

Staffs at Alabama, Baylor, Hawaii are using it. The high school version is the same workflow — just built for what a HS staff actually has time to manage.

The easiest way to understand it is to see it. Would a 15-minute walkthrough this week make sense?

coach_uses_excel.label: Coach uses Excel / Google Sheets
coach_uses_excel.script:
Got it — that's the most common workflow we see. Excel works, but it gets painful when the call sheet changes and now the wristband, practice script, and self-scout all need to stay aligned manually.

XIQ connects those pieces — play inventory, script, call sheet, wristband, self-scout — so the staff isn't updating four different files every time something changes.

I don't want to overpitch it on the phone. It's way easier to see live.

Would it be worth 15 minutes this week to show you what that workflow looks like?

book_demo.label: Book the demo
book_demo.script:
Good — I'll keep it tight, 15 minutes.

The walkthrough covers play inventory, script builder, call sheet, wristband printout, and self-scout.

Should we include your OC or QB coach too, or would you rather see it first?

[Confirm date/time and attendees]

athletic_director.label: Athletic Director
athletic_director.primary_goal: Get AD to refer to head coach, book a joint demo, or clarify budget/approval path
pain_points: Giving coaches better tools without adding staff
Budget fit — smaller spend vs helmets, travel, facilities
Competitive advantage without a major spend

opener.label: Opener — Program outcomes first
opener.script:
[AD Last Name], this is [Your Name] with XIQ — I'll be quick.

We work with football programs at Alabama, Baylor, Hawaii, and a lot of high school programs on one thing: giving the coaching staff a cleaner system for game-week prep — call sheets, scripts, wristbands, play inventory, self-scout — all in one place instead of scattered across Excel and PDFs.

Before I go straight to the football coach, I wanted to ask: when the football program evaluates a tool like this, does it come through you on the budget side, or does the head coach handle it independently?
opener.note: Lead with college social proof before routing. The budget question makes the AD feel like a decision-maker, not a receptionist.

ad_involved.label: AD is involved in budget/approval
ad_involved.script:
That makes sense — usually it's a head coach and AD conversation.

The coach evaluates whether it actually helps the staff. You help with approval or budget if there's a fit.

To be clear: this is not a Hudl replacement. Hudl is film. XIQ is the planning workflow around the film — scripts, call sheets, wristbands, play inventory, self-scout.

Would it make more sense to do a short walkthrough with you and Coach [Last Name] together, or should I show him first and bring you in if there's interest?

ad_routes_to_coach.label: AD routes to coach
ad_routes_to_coach.script:
Absolutely — that's usually the right next step.

Is Coach [Last Name] the best person, or should I start with the offensive coordinator?

Would it be okay if I mention I spoke with you briefly?

[If yes]: Perfect — what's the best email or number for him?

ad_what_does_it_do.label: AD asks what XIQ does
ad_what_does_it_do.script:
XIQ is a football operations platform for the coaching staff.

It helps them keep play inventory, weekly practice script, call sheet, wristband, and self-scout organized in one system.

Staffs at Alabama, Baylor, Hawaii use it. Most football staffs have film handled through Hudl — but the rest of game-week prep still lives in Excel, PDFs, and manual wristband templates. XIQ cleans that up and gives the staff one source of truth.

The best person to evaluate the football side would be Coach [Last Name]. Should I start with him, or would you want to be on the walkthrough too?

ad_objection_hudl.label: AD says they already have Hudl
ad_objection_hudl.script:
Totally — most schools do. We're not replacing Hudl.

Hudl is film. XIQ supports the workflow around the film — call sheets, scripts, wristbands, play inventory, and self-scout.

Most football staffs still manage that part manually even if they use Hudl every day.

Would Coach [Last Name] be the right person to evaluate that workflow?

branches.trigger: I want to see it too

ad_objection_no_budget.label: AD says no budget
ad_objection_no_budget.script:
Understood — I know athletic departments have to be careful.

Compared to helmets, travel, uniforms, or facilities, this is a much smaller spend. And the real question is whether Coach [Last Name] thinks it saves the staff enough time to be worth it.

The first step is just seeing whether he thinks it helps. Should I show him first and only bring you back in if there's interest?

ad_objection_send_info.label: AD says send info
ad_objection_send_info.script:
Absolutely — I'll send a short overview.

Should I frame it more as a football staff efficiency tool, a game-planning tool, or a program organization tool?

[Let AD answer]

Got it. And should I include Coach [Last Name] on that email?

[Then use the trial close]: Honestly this is one of those tools that's way faster to understand live than on a PDF. What if I send the overview and we put a 15-minute hold on the calendar — if it's not relevant after you read it, cancel it, no hard feelings. But if it looks interesting, we're already on the calendar. Does [specific day] work?

branches.trigger: Just send the info for now

ad_objection_not_my_area.label: AD says not my area
ad_objection_not_my_area.script:
No problem — who usually handles software or football program tools? The head coach, booster club, district, or someone else?

[Let them answer]

Got it — would Coach [Last Name] be the best place to start?

ad_book_joint_demo.label: Book joint AD + Coach demo
ad_book_joint_demo.script:
Great — the walkthrough is short. We'd show how the football staff manages play inventory, builds practice scripts, organizes call sheets, prints wristbands, and reviews self-scout from one place.

Would you prefer to loop in Coach [Last Name] on the same call, or should I get him on separately first?

objection_not_interested.label: Not interested
objection_not_interested.script: No problem. Just so I don't keep bothering you — is that because your staff already has the call sheet, script, wristband, and self-scout workflow solved, or is it just bad timing?

objection_ni_solved.label: They say it's already solved
objection_ni_solved.script:
Respect that — what are you using today?

[Listen and log the tool/system for CRM]

objection_ni_timing.label: Bad timing
objection_ni_timing.script:
Makes sense. When do you usually look at tools like this — spring, summer, before camp, or after the season?

[Log timing and set follow-up date]

objection_we_use_hudl.label: We use Hudl
objection_we_use_hudl.script:
Totally — almost everyone does. We're not replacing Hudl.

Hudl is film. XIQ is the planning layer around it — play inventory, call sheet, script, wristband, self-scout.

Are you still using Excel or Google Sheets for the call sheet and wristband side?

objection_have_system.label: We already have a system
objection_have_system.script:
That makes sense — most serious staffs do.

Is it a dedicated football planning system, or more of a combination of spreadsheets, Hudl notes, PDFs, and staff habits?

[Let them answer]

Got it. The reason XIQ exists is to connect those pieces — play inventory, script, call sheet, wristband, self-scout — so the staff isn't manually keeping everything aligned.

Worth taking a quick look to compare it against what you're doing now?

objection_send_info.label: Send me info
objection_send_info.script:
Absolutely. So I don't send you generic software stuff — what would be most relevant to your staff: call sheet, practice scripting, wristbands, or self-scout?

[Let them answer. Then:]

I'll send that over today. And honestly — this is one of those tools that's way faster to understand live than on a PDF.

What if we do this: I'll send the overview now, and we put a 15-minute hold on the calendar for [day]. If you look at the email and it's clearly not relevant, cancel it — no hard feelings. But if it looks interesting, we're already on the calendar.

Does [specific day] work for a quick hold?

objection_no_budget.label: No budget
objection_no_budget.script:
I hear you — most high school staffs are careful with budget.

The first step is just seeing whether it saves the staff enough time to matter. If it does, then we can talk through whether it fits under football budget, booster support, or athletic department budget.

Would it be worth looking at it first before we decide it's a budget issue?

objection_busy.label: We're busy
objection_busy.script: Completely understand. Let me ask one direct question: is your call sheet, wristband, practice script, and self-scout workflow already buttoned up — or is some of that still manual?

objection_call_after_season.label: Call me after the season
objection_call_after_season.script:
I hear that a lot. The problem is by August you'll be in camp, and by September you're in-season.

The staffs that get the most out of XIQ set it up in the summer — before the call sheet is due Week 1.

Can we at least do a 15-minute look now so you can decide after you've actually seen it?

not_the_coach.label: Reached wrong person — not the football coach
not_the_coach.script: No worries — I'm actually trying to reach Coach [Last Name], the head football coach. Is he someone you could point me to, or do you know the best way to reach him?

coach_not_football.label: Reached a coach but not football
coach_not_football.script: My apologies — I was actually trying to reach the head football coach. Do you know who that is or the best way to get in touch with him?

ad_not_involved_in_football.label: AD says football is totally separate from them
ad_not_involved_in_football.script:
Totally fair — I don't want to waste your time. Does the football program operate independently on tools and budget, or is there someone at the AD level I should loop in?

[If fully independent]: Got it — who's the best contact for the head football coach? Email or direct number?

office_no_coach_contact.label: Office staff can't or won't help
office_no_coach_contact.script: No problem at all — is there a general email I could use to reach out to Coach [Last Name] directly? Even the school's main football email works.
office_no_coach_contact.note: If they won't provide anything, log the school, note the gatekeeper block, and move to email outreach via school website.

re_engage_scripts.note: Use these for any contact who was previously warm — requested info, said call back later, or went cold after initial interest.

sent_info_no_response.label: Sent info, no response — follow-up call
sent_info_no_response.script:
Coach [Last Name], this is [Your Name] with XIQ — I sent you a short note about [call sheet / wristband / self-scout — whatever they flagged] a couple weeks back.

I'm not going to pitch you again — I just had one question: when you saw it, did it look like something that could actually help your staff, or was it just not the right fit?

[Let them answer — this is a temperature check, not a pitch]

reengage_havent_looked.label: They haven't looked at the info yet
reengage_havent_looked.script:
No worries — summer gets crazy fast.

Honestly, the info is fine but this is one of those things that's way faster to understand live. What if we just do a 15-minute look this week — if it's not relevant, you tell me in the first five minutes and we hang up.

Does [specific day] work?

said_call_back_later.label: Previously said 'call me back later'
said_call_back_later.script:
Coach [Last Name], [Your Name] with XIQ — you mentioned [spring / summer / after the season] would be a better time, so I wanted to circle back.

Still worth a quick look? The reason I'd push for now is that staffs who set this up before camp get a lot more out of it than ones who try to onboard in-season.

Would 15 minutes this week work?

demo_no_show.label: Booked demo but no-showed
demo_no_show.script:
Coach [Last Name], [Your Name] with XIQ — we had a 15-minute walkthrough on the calendar for [date/time] and I think something came up on your end.

No worries at all — happens every summer. Want to find a new time, or is the timing just not right?

ad_reengage_after_coach_referral.label: Re-engaging AD after coach showed interest
ad_reengage_after_coach_referral.script:
Hi [AD Last Name], [Your Name] with XIQ — I spoke with Coach [Last Name] and he took a look at XIQ, the football operations platform we work with at Alabama, Baylor, and Hawaii.

He thought it could be useful for the staff. I wanted to loop you in since you mentioned you're involved on the budget side.

Do you have 15 minutes this week to take a quick look together?

competitor_handling.note: Primary competitor is Google Sheets / Excel — inertia, not a named product. No dedicated HS football planning tool owns the market yet. If a coach claims a 'system,' it is almost always a combination of Sheets, PDFs, and Hudl.

google_sheets_as_competitor.label: Coach says Google Sheets is their system
google_sheets_as_competitor.script:
Yeah — that's honestly where most good staffs are right now. Google Sheets works, and I get why it's the default.

The problem we hear consistently is this: when the call sheet changes, now the wristband tab is wrong, the practice script doesn't match, and whoever is maintaining self-scout has to go update that separately. You're basically a spreadsheet admin during game week.

XIQ connects those pieces so when the play inventory changes, everything downstream updates. Call sheet, script, wristband, self-scout — one system.

Most coaches don't realize how much time the Sheets maintenance takes until they see what it looks like without it.

Would it be worth 15 minutes just to see the difference?

claims_dedicated_system.label: Coach claims they use a dedicated system (not Sheets)
claims_dedicated_system.script:
Interesting — what are you using?

[Let them answer and log it]

Got it. Does it handle all of it — play inventory, call sheet, practice script, wristband, and self-scout — or is it mainly one piece of that?
claims_dedicated_system.note: Most of the time they'll admit it only handles one piece. If they describe a true end-to-end system, log it as not a fit and note the tool name for competitive intel.

disposition_not_a_fit_competitor.label: True competitive loss — log for intel
disposition_not_a_fit_competitor.script:
Respect that — sounds like you guys are in good shape. What are you using? I like to know what's working out there.

[Log tool name. Thank them. Move on.]
disposition_not_a_fit_competitor.note: Log the competitor name in the CRM. This is competitive intelligence — track which tools come up repeatedly.

coach_voicemail.label: Coach voicemail
coach_voicemail.script:
Coach [Last Name], this is [Your Name] with XIQ.

We work with programs at Alabama, Baylor, Hawaii, and a lot of high school staffs on one thing: organizing game-week prep — play inventory, call sheets, practice scripts, wristbands, self-scout — so it's not scattered across Excel, Hudl notes, and PDFs.

Wanted to see if it would be worth showing you a quick 15-minute walkthrough. I'll send a short email too.

Again, [Your Name] with XIQ.

ad_voicemail.label: AD voicemail
ad_voicemail.script:
Hi [AD Last Name], this is [Your Name] with XIQ.

We help high school football programs give their coaching staff a cleaner system for weekly game prep — the same workflow staffs at Alabama, Baylor, and Hawaii are using.

I wanted to see whether this should go through you, Coach [Last Name], or someone else on the football staff.

I'll send a short email as well. Again, [Your Name] with XIQ.

coach_email.label: Post-call coach email
coach_email.subject: XIQ for [School] football
coach_email.body:
Coach [Last Name],

Good speaking with you.

XIQ helps football staffs organize the weekly offensive workflow in one place: play inventory, practice script, call sheet, wristband, and self-scout.

Staffs at Alabama, Baylor, and Hawaii are running this workflow. The high school version is the same system — built for what a HS staff actually has time to manage.

Worth a quick 15-minute look to see if it would fit how your staff operates?

Best,
[Your Name]

ad_email.label: Post-call AD email
ad_email.subject: XIQ / [School] football program
ad_email.body:
Hi [AD Last Name],

Good speaking with you.

XIQ is a football operations platform built for high school staffs. It helps coaches organize play inventory, practice scripts, call sheets, wristbands, and self-scout in one place — the same workflow used at Alabama, Baylor, and Hawaii.

This is not a Hudl replacement. It supports the weekly coaching workflow around game planning and staff organization.

I'd be happy to show Coach [Last Name] a short walkthrough and bring you in if there's interest.

Best,
[Your Name]

initial.script: Hi, I'm trying to reach Coach [Last Name], the head football coach. Is he available?

gatekeeper_what_regarding.script: It's regarding a football operations tool for call sheets, practice scripts, wristbands, and weekly game planning.

gatekeeper_email.script: No problem — what's the best email for Coach [Last Name]? And does he prefer calls at the school number or another line?

gatekeeper_timing.script: Totally understand — is there usually a better time to catch him? Before school, lunch, or after practice?

workflow: How are you building your call sheet right now?
Are wristbands managed in Excel or somewhere else?
Where does your play inventory live today?
Who owns the weekly practice script?
Do you have a clean way to go from play inventory to practice script to call sheet?
pain: What part of game-week prep is the most manual for your staff?
How long does it take to update your wristbands when the call sheet changes?
Is self-scout something you guys do weekly, or only when there's time?
Does your staff have one source of truth, or does each coach manage their own version?
How many coaches are touching the offensive plan during game week?
self_scout: Are you guys self-scouting by down, distance, personnel, formation, and concept right now?
Is that easy to pull, or is it more of a manual Sunday/Monday project?
timing: When do you usually evaluate new tools — spring, summer, before camp, or after the season?
Are you already preparing your install and offensive system for next season?

ad: Do football software requests usually go through you, the head coach, boosters, or the district?
Are you involved when coaches ask for tools that support their program?
Would this type of tool usually come from football budget, booster support, or athletic department budget?
Do you know if the football staff is still building call sheets and wristbands manually?

call_sequence.goal: Reach contact, run script, book demo
Leave persona-specific voicemail if no answer
Reference prior call/email — 'I left a quick note about XIQ — the system for call sheets, scripts, wristbands, self-scout. Quick question: are you still managing most of that manually?'
Short value email with one use case
Referral angle — 'Coach, should I be speaking with you on this, or is your OC the better person for call sheet and script workflow?'
